{{Short description|Organization for Human Brain Mapping award}}
{{Use mdy dates|date=March 2018}}
{{Infobox award
| name          = OHBM Replication Award
| image         = 
| image_upright = 
| caption       = 
| awarded_for   = A neuroimaging replication study of exceptional quality and impact.
| presenter     = [[Organization for Human Brain Mapping]] (OHBM)
| country       = 
| year          = 2017
| holder        = [[Helmet T. Karim]] for "Independent replication of advanced brain age in mild cognitive impairment and dementia: detection of future cognitive dysfunction" (awarded in 2024)
| website       = {{url|humanbrainmapping.org}}
}}

The '''OHBM Replication Award''' is an award presented annually by the [[Organization for Human Brain Mapping]] (OHBM). It is presented to a researcher in recognition of conducting and disseminating the results of a [[neuroimaging]] [[Reproducibility|replication study]] of exceptional quality and impact.

==Winners==
[[File:2018 OHBM Replication Award.jpg|thumb|Benedikt Sundermann receives the 2018 OHBM Replication Award]]

==History==
The award was originally conceived by [[Chris Gorgolewski]] as an attempt to elevate the status of replication studies, which were often considered not as prestigious as other scientific activities. Researchers focusing too much on novel discoveries instead of scrutinizing previously published findings was big contribution to [[reproducibility crisis]] in psychology.

The award has increased the likelihood of members of the neuroimaging community to conduct and disseminate results of replication studies<ref name="GorgolewskiNichols2018">{{cite journal|last1=Gorgolewski|first1=Krzysztof J.|last2=Nichols|first2=Thomas|last3=Kennedy|first3=David N.|last4=Poline|first4=Jean-Baptiste|last5=Poldrack|first5=Russell A.|title=Making replication prestigious|journal=Behavioral and Brain Sciences|volume=41|year=2018|article-number=e131 |issn=0140-525X|doi=10.1017/S0140525X18000663|pmid=31064546 |s2cid=147706022 }}</ref> and the procedure of running the award has been made publicly available<ref>{{Cite journal|last5=Poldrack|first5=Russell|last4=Poline|first4=Jean-Baptiste|last3=N. Kennedy|first3=David|last2=Nichols|first2=Thomas|last1=Gorgolewski|first1=Krzysztof|date=2017-11-03|title=Replication Award Creation Kit|url=https://figshare.com/articles/Replication_Award_Creation_Kit/5567083|language=en|doi=10.6084/m9.figshare.5567083.v1}}</ref> in hope other academic communities could implement similar awards.
